explicit_text: South Jacksonville is where the old 'Red Bank' plantation manor stood, right at the edge of its corporate limits. This area transformed when the estate was subdivided into what became Colonial Manor, a neighborhood popular for those wanting to live near city conveniences but away from the busy city center. I recall how the property line was even extended into the St. Johns River thanks to modern dredging techniques.
implicit_text: This area is where the old plantation manor once stood, right at the edge of its corporate limits. The place transformed when the estate was subdivided into a neighborhood popular for those wanting to live near city conveniences but away from the busy city center. I recall how the property line was even extended into the nearby river thanks to modern dredging techniques.

explicit_text: The St. Johns River was a defining feature of the old plantation called 'Red Bank,' which had a seven-mile frontage on it. When the land was developed into Colonial Manor, sand and silt were dredged from the riverbed to extend the property line over a hundred feet into the river. Originally, the manor house was just a stone's throw from the riverbank, but now it sits six blocks away due to these changes.

explicit_text: My family has been long identified with Duval County, owning the 'Red Bank' plantation manor for over a hundred years. The property and its history are deeply tied to this county, and it was here that the old estate was eventually sold and developed into the Colonial Manor subdivision. Living in Duval County connects me to a rich heritage and the growth of the Jacksonville area.

explicit_text: Colonial Manor came into being when the old 'Red Bank' plantation was subdivided into building lots. It was named after the historic manor house and became a sought-after neighborhood for those wanting to live away from the busy city but still close to modern conveniences like running water and electric light. I live here now, and the old manor house stands as a dignified reminder of the area's colonial past.

explicit_text: Ridgewood Road is the street where the old 'Red Bank' manor house faces, maintaining its solid square front as it did in the past. The sand trail that once led to the front porch was a popular meeting place for neighboring plantation owners. Today, the house still stands dignified on Ridgewood Road, surrounded by age-old live oaks and magnolia trees.

explicit_text: I spent my childhood on a Georgia plantation, which gave me a deep appreciation for homes with spacious rooms and high ceilings like the old 'Red Bank' manor. When I purchased and began restoring the manor house here in Florida, it felt like coming back to a familiar style of living. Also, my longtime helper Janis was brought down from Georgia when I married nearly twenty years ago, and her family roots go back to that state as well.

explicit_text: I am a member of the Story Tellers League, and we were hosting a tea at my home to raise funds for our national convention scheduled to be held in Jacksonville next fall. The event was a public affair, expected to draw around a hundred people, and involved a decorating committee preparing the rooms with flowers and greenery. Being part of this organization connects me to a community that values storytelling and cultural events.

It's rewarding to see him involved in such wholesome and character-building experiences.

explicit_text: The Jacksonville Gas Company has not extended gas service to the southside where we live because it would be too expensive to lay pipes under the St. Johns River or build an auxiliary plant. As a result, our kitchen uses all electrical appliances, which suits our modern lifestyle. This limitation has influenced how we equip and run our household.
